site.name: HIP - Florianópolis
site.tagline: Your practical guide to getting started in Florianópolis

home.heroTitle: Just arrived in Floripa? We'll help you find your way.
home.heroSubtitle: A simple step-by-step trail with everything you need to sort out in your first days in the city: documents, health, transport, work, and more.
home.aboutBody: Arriving in a new city means handling many things at once, and it's not always clear where to start. This portal gathers practical, free information for immigrants already in Florianópolis or preparing to arrive.
aboutMoreBody: This portal is the result of a collaboration between UNICESUSC and Círculos de Hospitalidade, bringing together the university and civil society around one goal: welcoming those who arrive in Florianópolis.
UNICESUSC provides the academic foundation and extension project structure. Círculos de Hospitalidade bring the day-to-day experience of welcoming immigrants and refugees in the city.
The result is an open, free guide built by students. Information is organized to ease first-day anxiety and help you navigate with more independence.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Stories and practical guides to help you feel at home in Florianópolis.
blog.intro: Here we go beyond step-by-step: welcoming introductory texts about daily life in the city — transport, health, work, education — and everything that helps you put down roots. Open content written for newcomers.
blog.availableInPt: This article is not yet available in English. Showing the Portuguese version.

contacts.featuredBody: Are you a migrant or refugee living in Santa Catarina? The Círculos de Hospitalidade team offers free welcome and guidance, in person and online. Register on their page. Contact is via WhatsApp.

about.title: About the project
about.intro: HIP - Florianópolis is an open, free portal that organizes practical information for anyone arriving (or preparing to arrive) in Florianópolis. The goal is to reduce first-day chaos: documents, transport, health, work, housing, and everything that stalls without centralized guidance in more than one language.
about.academicLead: Connecting Generations and Driving the Future and Digital Agency
about.academicBodyBeforeUnicesusc: This portal was developed as part of a CESUTech extension project, linked to Centro Universitário Cesusc (
about.academicBodyAfterCirculos: . Despite its academic origin, the focus is practical: a real public utility tool for migrants in the city.
about.disclaimerBeforeContact: Information here is initial guidance and may change over time. Always confirm with official agencies before making an important decision. If you find something wrong or outdated, please

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about using the HIP - Florianópolis portal.
faq.stillNeedHelpBody: If you didn't find what you were looking for, check the support contacts in Florianópolis.

items.a: Yes. HIP - Florianópolis is an open, free project. You pay nothing to access any information.

items.q: Do I need to create an account?
items.a: No. All content is available without login. We don't have accounts yet — you can browse everything freely.

items.q: What languages is the portal available in?
items.a: Portuguese, Spanish, French, and English. You can switch language anytime using the selector at the top of the page. Some editorial content is still being translated — in those cases, we show the Portuguese version with a notice.
We gather and organize information from public sources as initial guidance. It may change over time, so always confirm with official agencies before making an important decision.

items.q: How does the orientation trail work?
items.a: It's a step-by-step path through the most important first-day topics: documents, health, transport, work, and more. Follow the suggested order or go straight to what you need now.

items.q: I found incorrect or outdated information. What should I do?
items.a: Let us know. Use the "let us know" button on the About page or the contacts listed there. Your feedback helps keep everything accurate.

items.q: I need in-person support. Can the portal help?
items.a: Yes. On the Contacts page and Map you'll find public agencies, NGOs, and support services in Florianópolis, with addresses and contact details.
It's an academic extension project maintained by volunteers, in partnership with Círculos de Hospitalidade. Code and content are open to contributors.

common.contentFallback: This content is not yet available in English. Showing the Portuguese version.

novidades.title: Free courses and opportunities
novidades.subtitle: We gather free courses, study tracks and training opportunities for people building their lives in Florianópolis. The list is reviewed by people before being published.

novidades.guideEvPtOnly: The Bradesco Virtual School route (free certificate) is in Portuguese and requires a CPF. Use Microsoft Learn above if you don't have that document yet.
